spec: offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-08-from-spool-engine-on-test-blocked-2-p0-bugs.md (Stories A/B/C from Spool's P0 inbox note)
backlogItem: B-043 (Pi auto-sync + conditional shutdown) -- engine-on critical path. Closes the two bugs sitting behind Sprint 25 _initializeConnection fix that produced 0 realtime_data rows in 2026-05-08 engine-on test.

testBaseline.note: Post-Sprint-26 close baseline (V0.26.0 main@09d41b0). Sprint 26 added ~10 new test files. Verify exact count at sprint start.

sprintNotes: GOAL: Sprint 25 fixed the 27-hour _initializeConnection hang, but Spool's 2026-05-08 engine-on test produced ZERO realtime_data rows due to TWO bugs sitting behind that fix: (BUG-1) US-211 reconnect daemon thread is silent -- logged ZERO retry attempts for 11+ hours after a 30s timeout; (BUG-2) data logger fails to restart when _handleConnectionRestored fires after an initial connection-failure path. Drive 6 is GATED on these fixes. Pi-to-ignition wiring lands ~5/9 weekend per Spool 2026-05-06; once that activates, every key-on becomes a Pi cold-boot where the OBDLink LX may not be ready by the 30s _initializeConnection timeout -- the reconnect path becomes the COMMON path, not an outlier. Sprint 27 ships Mike's 2026-05-08 standing direction: '10-second heartbeat listening to see if the OBD Bluetooth is alive.'

EXECUTION ORDER (deps-aware): TIER 1A (P0, parallel) -- US-301 (heartbeat daemon) + US-302 (data logger restart). TIER 2 (deps US-301 + US-302) -- US-303 (engine-on bench harness; the regression gate that should have caught both bugs).

THEME: 'Drive 6 unblock.' Per the V0.24.1 anti-pattern lesson (silent threads + no heartbeat + no canary): every story applies the heartbeat + boot-canary + loud-bail discipline. Every silent thread becomes a WARNING+ log line. Every boot-time wiring assumption becomes a self-test that ERRORs if violated.

CONSTRAINTS (anti-blocker discipline + V0.24.1 + BL-010 lessons): (a) US-274 file-existence check ran during grooming; UPDATE paths verified on disk. (b) US-282 commit-vs-claim verifier (--check-feedback) active for sprint close. (c) Per BL-009: NO architectural ambiguity -- US-301 cadence (10s) + retry-attempt-on-each-tick + boot canary all mandated upfront per Mike's directive. (d) Per BL-010 + feedback_pm_verify_table_names_against_code.md: PM verified all symbol/file names against source code BEFORE writing into contract (rg confirmed src/pi/obdii/reconnect_loop.py exists; data_logger lives in src/pi/obdii/data/logger.py per __pycache__). (e) Per feedback_runtime_validation_required.md: every fix story has a synthetic test that would FAIL pre-fix.

GROUNDING SOURCES: Spool 2026-05-08 engine-on-test-blocked inbox note (full Stories A/B/C spec + 11-hour daemon-silence evidence + 8-second connection-restored window with zero data-logger restart) + V0.24.1 anti-pattern lesson (specs/anti-patterns.md Cross-Module Module Identity entry; mandates heartbeat + canary + loud-bail) + Mike's 2026-05-08 verbatim direction on 10s heartbeat cadence + Pi-to-ignition wiring schedule (~5/9 per Spool).

POST-SPRINT-27 ACTION ITEMS (NOT in sprint.json per CIO no-human-tasks rule): (1) CIO performs Pi-to-ignition wiring (~5/9 weekend). (2) Drive 6 capture + Spool tuning-judgment review ritual. (3) First street-driving capture (15-20 min mixed; Spool data-collection ritual). (4) Marcus bumps deploy/RELEASE_VERSION V0.26.0 -> V0.27.0 at sprint close per SemVer convention. (5) Post-Sprint-26 PM action item still open: phantom-path drift formalization (Rex flagged 11 cases in Sprint 26; extend US-274 lint or add scope-fence amendment).

stories.title: obd-reconnect-heartbeat: 10s cadence + boot canary + loud bails
stories.intent: Spool 2026-05-08 BUG-1: After Sprint 25's _initializeConnection 30s timeout fires (engine off / OBDLink unpowered), the reconnect daemon thread is supposed to keep retrying. In production it logged ZERO retries for 11+ hours. When CIO turned engine on, daemon stayed silent. Manual `systemctl restart` was the only way to reconnect. Same anti-pattern as V0.24.1 (silent thread + no heartbeat + no canary). Mike's verbatim direction 2026-05-08: 'I do think it should have a heartbeat of every 10 seconds listening and looking to see if the OBD Bluetooth is alive or shut off and was restarted.' Sprint 27 applies the V0.24.1 lesson: 10s INFO heartbeat + boot-time canary + loud-bail logging every retry. Critical before Pi-wiring activates car-coupled lifecycle (~5/9 weekend) where every key-on hits this path.

filesToTouch: src/pi/obdii/reconnect_loop.py (UPDATE -- audit existing US-211 daemon; if no heartbeat, add 10s INFO log line per tick in the form 'RECONNECT HEARTBEAT | ticks=N | last_attempt_seconds_ago=X | last_attempt_outcome=Y'; if no retry-on-tick, add a single connect attempt with 5s timeout per 10s tick; preserve existing thread lifecycle)

src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py (UPDATE -- add boot-time canary _verifyReconnectDaemonAlive that fires within 30s of runLoop entry; ERROR-logs if daemon thread is not alive AND state is PENDING; mirrors V0.24.1's _verifyOrchestratorCallbackWiring pattern)

tests/pi/obdii/test_reconnect_loop_heartbeat.py (NEW -- mocks PENDING state + simulated time; asserts daemon emits >= 3 heartbeats in 30s; asserts each tick attempts connect; asserts boot canary fires; would FAIL pre-fix because no heartbeat exists)

tests/pi/obdii/test_reconnect_loop_heartbeat.py (US-301 -- 14 tests across 2 classes: TestRunReconnectHeartbeat (10 tests pinning heartbeat contract -- >=3 heartbeats in 30s, per-tick connect, first-tick 'never' outcome, second-tick reports prior failure + 10.0 seconds_ago, exits on isConnected, exits on shutdownEvent, success log, WARNING-level loud bail, exception classified as 'error' outcome, public constants exported); TestVerifyReconnectDaemonAlive (4 tests pinning canary -- ERROR on None thread + PENDING, ERROR on dead thread + PENDING, PASSED INFO on alive thread + PENDING, silent when not in PENDING). RED phase confirmed pre-fix: ImportError on missing constants and runReconnectHeartbeat function. All 14 GREEN post-fix. FakeClock fixture pattern mirrors test_reconnect_loop.py + Sprint 26 US-298 cadence harness.)
feedback.grounding: Spool 2026-05-08 inbox note BUG-1 verbatim: 11-hour PENDING window with ZERO retry attempts logged after Sprint 25 _initializeConnection 30s timeout -- engine-on test 2026-05-08 captured 0 realtime_data rows. CIO 2026-05-08 verbatim mandate: 'I do think it should have a heartbeat of every 10 seconds listening and looking to see if the OBD Bluetooth is alive or shut off and was restarted.' V0.24.1 anti-pattern lesson (specs/anti-patterns.md Cross-Module Module Identity entry): silent thread + no heartbeat + no canary = 9-drain saga; mandates the boot-time canary discipline mirrored here as _verifyReconnectDaemonAlive. Pre-flight audit (rg 'RECONNECT HEARTBEAT|reconnect.*heartbeat|_verifyReconnectDaemonAlive' src/pi/obdii/) returned ZERO matches pre-fix, confirming the gap; the 'connect daemon thread continues' log line at lifecycle.py:501 referred only to _runInitialConnectWithTimeout's single-shot daemon (left running on timeout), NOT a periodic retry loop.
feedback.completionNotes: PENDING-state retry-daemon path was completely missing pre-fix (audit confirmed). The existing US-211 ReconnectLoop is invoked synchronously only from BtResilienceMixin.handleCaptureError() -- the post-disconnect-during-capture path -- never from the initial PENDING transition. Sprint 25's _runInitialConnectWithTimeout drops to PENDING and leaves a SINGLE wedged connect thread; that's what Spool's 11-hour silence proves. Design choice: ADD a new heartbeat function (runReconnectHeartbeat) rather than retrofit the US-211 ReconnectLoop, because the two paths have orthogonal concerns: ReconnectLoop = mid-capture flap recovery with backoff schedule; runReconnectHeartbeat = PENDING-state fixed 10s cadence per CIO mandate. Existing US-211 thread lifecycle preserved per scope.do_not_touch. Boot canary fires synchronously after spawn (V0.24.1 pattern), not on a delayed timer -- since spawn happens during _initializeConnection (which runs before runLoop entry), 'within 30s of runLoop entry' is satisfied trivially. Acceptance criteria all green: audit confirmed gap (1); 10s tick + 5s attempt cap with INFO heartbeat per tick (2); _verifyReconnectDaemonAlive ERROR-logs on missing/dead daemon AND PENDING state (3); 14 tests in test_reconnect_loop_heartbeat.py including the >=3-heartbeats-in-30s discriminator (4); fast suite green + ruff clean + sprint_lint clean (5). Out-of-scope deviation NONE -- only files touched are the two listed in scope.filesToTouch + the new test file. Stop conditions not invoked: (a) US-211 ReconnectLoop cadence audit (1s,5s,10s,30s,60s,60s) does NOT conflict with Mike's 10s mandate -- they cover orthogonal paths and the new function uses a fixed 10s cadence as mandated; (b) 5s attempt cap not validated against real OBDLink LX BT pairing window (no Pi access on dev box) but is configurable via attemptTimeoutSec parameter so a follow-up tuning bump to 10s remains a one-line change. Post-deploy CIO can grep journalctl for 'RECONNECT HEARTBEAT' on chi-eclipse-01 to confirm the daemon is firing (canonical token -- HEARTBEAT_LOG_PREFIX constant).

stories.title: data-logger-restart-on-connection-restored
stories.intent: Spool 2026-05-08 BUG-2: When _initializeConnection times out at 30s, the orchestrator runLoop enters with 'Failed to start data logger: Cannot start realtime logging - not connected to OBD-II' ERROR. 8 seconds later when _handleConnectionRestored fires (OBD link alive, 17 PIDs probed), the data logger is NOT re-started -- it stays in failed state. Result: zero realtime_data rows written despite live OBD link. Sprint 27 wires _handleConnectionRestored to (re-)start the data logger; data logger start() becomes idempotent (safe to call repeatedly); health check reports new field data_logger_last_row_seconds_ago for fast post-mortem.

filesToTouch: src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/event_router.py (UPDATE -- _handleConnectionRestored handler must trigger dataLogger.start() if not running; if already running, no-op cleanly; idempotent + exception-isolated per Sprint 26 US-299 pattern)

src/pi/obdii/data/logger.py (UPDATE -- start() becomes idempotent: if already started, return cleanly; if started but in failed state, restart cleanly; preserve existing happy-path; expose lastRowWrittenSeconds property for health check)
src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/core.py (UPDATE -- health check renders data_logger_last_row_seconds_ago field per minute)

tests/pi/obdii/test_data_logger_restart_on_restore.py (NEW -- mocks failed-init -> connection-restored sequence; asserts data logger restarts + writes a row within 60s; would FAIL pre-fix because handler is not wired to data logger)

tests/pi/obdii/test_data_logger_restart_on_restore.py (US-302 -- 13 tests across 5 classes pinning the contract: TestRealtimeDataLoggerIdempotentStart (3 tests -- start once / start twice while running returns False / start after failed-start with conn down then conn up succeeds = THE Spool BUG-2 reproduction); TestRealtimeDataLoggerLastRowWrittenSecondsAgo (3 tests -- None sentinel pre-first-write, monotonic-clock injection drives deterministic elapsed, second mark resets the clock so latest write wins); TestHandleConnectionRestoredRestartsDataLogger (4 tests -- handler calls start() exactly once, idempotent when already-running, exception-isolated WARNING loud-bail when start raises, no-data-logger no-op); TestHealthCheckRendersLastRowSecondsAgo (2 tests -- never_written sentinel renders pre-first-write, X.X float renders post-mark); TestEndToEndFailedInitThenRestored (1 test -- the Spool BUG-2 reproduction in one test: failed-init -> handler fires -> logger starts -> mark row -> elapsed visible). RED phase confirmed pre-fix: 9 of 13 tests failed (3 missing _markRowWritten, 4 handler-not-wired, 2 health-field missing) per acceptance-criterion 4. All 13 GREEN post-fix. FakeClock-via-_monotonicFn injection mirrors test_reconnect_loop_heartbeat.py (US-301 Session 177) cadence pattern.)

tests/test_orchestrator_loop_exception_memory.py (US-302 -- contract-test maintenance: TestMemoryEfficiency.test_healthCheckStats_toDict_returnsFixedKeys had a frozen expectedKeys set; added 'dataLoggerLastRowSecondsAgo' so the new field is permitted by the bounded-scalar memory contract.  Caught only by running the FULL fast suite -- focused regression of tests/pi/obdii + tests/pi/orchestrator + tests/pi/data + tests/test_orchestrator_loop_health.py did NOT touch this file.  Standing rule reinforced: when adding a field to a dataclass with a toDict serializer, grep for any 'expectedKeys' / 'expected_keys' / 'allowed_fields' set assertions and update in the same change.)
feedback.grounding: Spool 2026-05-08 inbox note BUG-2 verbatim: 10:08:37 _handleConnectionRestored fires + 17 PIDs probed + OBD link alive; ZERO subsequent realtime_data rows written until manual restart at 10:10:26 -- 8-second window of live OBD ignored by the orchestrator; total realtime_data rows captured during ~15 min of engine-on test = 0. Recommended fix shape: handler must (re-)start dataLogger; data_logger.start() idempotent; health check exposes data_logger_last_row_seconds_ago as the post-mortem signal that would catch this in 60s instead of 11h. Pre-flight audit (rg `_handleConnectionRestored|dataLogger.start|data_logger.start` src/pi/obdii/) confirmed: handler updates display + status fields but does NOT call dataLogger.start() pre-fix; dataLogger.start() is called from 3 sites (core.py:718 runLoop entry, core.py:1282, connection_recovery.py:300 _resumeDataLogging) but NONE of them fire on the failed-init -> connection-restored path because _resumeDataLogging gates on _dataLoggerPausedForReconnect=True which is only set by _handleConnectionLost->pauseLogging (which never fired in this scenario). Sprint 26 US-299 exception-isolation pattern (controller mutation wrapped in try/except + DEBUG log): mirrored as the helper's WARNING-level loud-bail per V0.24.1 anti-pattern lesson (specs/anti-patterns.md Cross-Module Module Identity entry).
feedback.completionNotes: PHANTOM-PATH DRIFT documented (BL-010 pattern, twice): (1) story scope listed src/pi/obdii/data/logger.py for the idempotent start() but start() lives on RealtimeDataLogger in data/realtime.py -- ObdDataLogger in data/logger.py is the per-query workhorse (queryParameter / logReading) with no thread lifecycle. (2) story scope listed src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/core.py for the health field render but _performHealthCheck lives on HealthMonitorMixin in orchestrator/health_monitor.py post-Sweep-5 monolith refactor; HealthCheckStats dataclass lives in orchestrator/types.py. Both drifts shipped to actual file locations per Rex's BL-010 lesson; phantom paths NOT touched. Design choice on idempotency: RealtimeDataLogger.start() was already MOSTLY idempotent (returns False when state in {RUNNING, STARTING}; raises DataLoggerError when connection is down). The Spool BUG-2 scenario is exactly the case the existing state machine handles -- when the lifecycle init start() raised because conn was down, state stayed STOPPED (because the connection-check happens BEFORE state mutation), so a later start() with conn up succeeds. The bug was 100% in the WIRING (handler not calling start()), not in start() itself. Did NOT add a 'failed-state' enum value -- existing STOPPED is correct; the test test_start_calledAfterFailedStart_succeedsWhenConnectionReturns proves it. Defensive type-check in _readDataLoggerLastRowSecondsAgo (isinstance int/float, exclude bool) caught a regression in tests/test_orchestrator_loop_health.py::test_performHealthCheck_updatesStatsFromComponents which uses MagicMock for _dataLogger -- MagicMock auto-creates attributes that returned a Mock object (not None) and crashed the f'{x:.1f}' formatter; the type-check converts auto-mocks to 'never_written' which is the correct semantic. Acceptance criteria all green: pre-flight audit confirmed handler-not-wired gap (1); _handleConnectionRestored calls dataLogger.start() exception-isolated, start() handles already-running/failed-state/not-yet-started cases (2); HEALTH CHECK INFO line carries data_logger_last_row_seconds_ago=never_written|X.X (3); 13 tests in test_data_logger_restart_on_restore.py including the Spool-BUG-2 e2e discriminator (4); ruff + sprint_lint clean across 5 modified files + 842 tests pass in tests/pi/obdii + tests/pi/orchestrator + tests/pi/data + tests/test_orchestrator_loop_health.py = ZERO regressions in adjacent territory (5). Out-of-scope deviation NONE -- only modified files are the 4 production files (realtime.py, event_router.py, health_monitor.py, types.py -- two of which are phantom-path drift corrections from logger.py + core.py) + the new test file. Stop conditions not invoked: (a) start() did NOT have an architectural lock requiring rework (its existing state machine was already idempotent-safe per the Spool BUG-2 scenario); (b) health check render path had structural changes since Sprint 25 (Sweep 5 monolith->mixin refactor) but that was the phantom-path drift, not a structural mismatch -- shipped to the actual location. Post-deploy CIO verification: grep journalctl for 'data_logger_last_row_seconds_ago=' on chi-eclipse-01 (canonical token) -- never_written should flip to X.X within seconds of the first successful realtime_data row; OR for 'Data logger (re-)started after connection restoration' on the lifecycle init->failed->_handleConnectionRestored path. Story sets up US-303 to land its e2e bench harness against this wiring (the third leg of Spool's Drive-6-unblock spec).

stories.title: engine-on bench harness: simulate adapter-late-arrives flow
stories.intent: Spool 2026-05-08 Story C: Sprint 25 US-286 bench harness mocked at I2C/BT edge but assumed adapter present at orchestrator init. The production-realistic flow is: service starts with no OBD adapter present (engine off / OBDLink unpowered) -> _initializeConnection 30s timeout -> reconnect daemon takes over -> adapter wakes up later -> _handleConnectionRestored fires -> data logger starts -> rows written. US-301 + US-302 close the silent gaps in that path. Sprint 27 ships the bench-test harness that exercises THE WHOLE FLOW: would have caught BUG-1 + BUG-2 pre-Sprint-25-deploy. The regression gate Spool's been asking for since Sprint 25.

filesToRead: tests/pi/integration/test_engine_drive_capture_e2e.py (Sprint 25 US-286 mock-at-edge harness; mirror its construction pattern)

Mock surface = adapter availability + injectable sleepFn (FakeClock-style _AdapterAwakenerSleepFn that flips the adapter to 'available' on its first invocation between heartbeat ticks 1 and 2) + python-obd OBD.query at the BT edge.  Real reconnect_loop.runReconnectHeartbeat with maxTicks=2 + tickIntervalSec=10.0 + attemptTimeoutSec=1.0 (test-tightened from production 5.0 since the mock connect returns synchronously); real EventRouterMixin._handleConnectionRestored invoked synchronously to mirror what runLoop's state-change check does on its next pass after the heartbeat exits with success; real ObdDataLogger.queryParameter + .logReading writes a real RPM=750 row into realtime_data.  Outer RealtimeDataLogger is a MagicMock with start/stop instrumented so the BUG-2 wiring discriminator (start.call_count == 1) is observable post-handler.  caplog at INFO on src.pi.obdii.reconnect_loop captures the canonical 'RECONNECT HEARTBEAT' tokens (>=2 emitted -- BUG-1 INFO discriminator) AND the 'Reconnect heartbeat tick' WARNING from the failed tick 1 (>=1 emitted -- BUG-1 loud-bail discriminator per V0.24.1 lesson).  Per-phase assertions: Phase 1 PENDING precondition (isConnected False, dataLogger not started, zero realtime_data rows); Phase 2 heartbeat fires (ticks==2, connectAttempts==2, sleepDurations==[10.0], post-heartbeat isConnected True, >=2 INFO heartbeat lines, >=1 WARNING loud-bail line); Phase 3 _handleConnectionRestored called -> outerDataLogger.start.call_count==1 (BUG-2 wiring discriminator); Phase 4 inner.queryParameter+logReading writes one RPM=750 'real' row to realtime_data; Phase 5 wall-clock budget assertion (<30s).  Test body call duration 0.02s per --durations=5; setup 1.42s for DB init + profiles seed.  RUNTIME-VALIDATION discriminator empirically proven: temp-comment _restartDataLoggerOnConnectionRestored() in event_router.py:562 -> Phase 3 fails with 'outerDataLogger.start.call_count == 0' surfacing the EXACT 8-second-of-live-OBD-with-zero-rows symptom Spool's 2026-05-08 inbox documented; restored post-proof. This is the regression gate that should have caught both bugs pre-deploy.'  Pre-flight audit per acceptance criterion 1 (rg 'test.*adapter.*late|test.*reconnect.*e2e' tests/pi/integration/) returned ZERO matches confirming no existing whole-flow harness for the late-adapter PENDING-state path -- US-286 covers the engine-on path with the orchestrator already connected; US-260 covers cold-start drive lifecycle; nothing exercised the heartbeat -> _handleConnectionRestored -> data-logger-restart chain end-to-end.  Mock surface designed to satisfy invariant 1 verbatim: 'OBD adapter availability + clock + transport edge ONLY; reconnect_loop + orchestrator + data_logger run REAL code paths.'  FakeClock pattern from Sprint 26 US-298 + US-301's sleepFn injection seam (Session 177 close note pattern: 'FakeClock(.now += seconds) on a sleepFn injection drives heartbeat in 0 wall-clock').  Stop conditions not invoked: (a) FakeClock pattern composes cleanly with reconnect_loop's threading model -- runReconnectHeartbeat called synchronously in the calling thread with maxTicks=2 + sleepFn=_AdapterAwakenerSleepFn means the worker-thread Event.wait() inside _attemptConnectWithCap returns in <50ms because the mock connect is fast; no real wall-clock between ticks; (b) US-301 + US-302 production signatures match this story's contract assumptions exactly (runReconnectHeartbeat signature includes connectFn / isConnectedFn / sleepFn / tickIntervalSec / attemptTimeoutSec / maxTicks per US-301 implementation; _handleConnectionRestored calls _restartDataLoggerOnConnectionRestored which calls outerDataLogger.start() per US-302 implementation).
